steps: Gather the five spreadsheets — wherever they actually live, including that one person's Downloads folder — into a single folder on your computer.
Open Claude Code and point it at that folder.
Ask it to find every figure the LCSR needs across those files and build one worksheet, with each number traced back to its source file and cell.
Tell it plainly: if a number isn't in any of the files, leave the cell empty and flag it for you — never estimate or guess a fill-in.
Ask it to recompute each total straight from the raw file and show its arithmetic next to the number it reported.
Chase down the real number yourself for every flagged empty cell before the report goes anywhere.
Have the person who owns the LCSR filing review and sign the consolidated worksheet before it's submitted.
verify: Pick three numbers on the consolidated worksheet, open the source file and cell it cites, and check the number by eye — if any of the three don't match their cited cell, the whole worksheet needs a re-check before anyone signs it.
